Title: Info
Text:
Now, we can validate a JSON string using a JSON format. This is the same principle as a xml file with its DTD.


1. We have a new command, JSON Validate:

oResult <- JSON Validate ( vJson ; vSchema )

JSON Validate is a 4D command which is validate a "vJson" JSON text against to a "vSchema" JSON schema. The command returns an object with the status (true or false) and an error list (code, description, line, offset).


2. To find the position of an object attribute with the information "line" and "offset", we have updated the JSON Parse command with a star option:

vResult <- JSON Parse ( vJson ; * )

If you use the star option, the "__symbols" attribute is added:

{
"alpha": 4552,
"beta": "text1",
"__symbols": {
"alpha": {
"line": 2,
"offset": 4 },
"beta": {
"line": 2,
"offset": 4 }
}
}


3. Thus, to validate and to find the possible error in your JSON, you need to pass the "__symbols" attribute to the JSON Validate command. You write the following code:

oResult <- JSON Validate ( JSON Parse ( vJson ; * ) ; vSchema )

If you do not pass the "__symbols" attribute, the line and offset are ignored.

Title: Create schema
Text:
I want to validate my JSON string that contains the information of a person. Thus, I create a schema named "Person" with the following characteristics:
* firstName attribute is a string and mandatory
* lastName attribute is a string and mandatory
* gender attribute is an enumerated type with M for male or F for female
* birthday attribute is an object that contains the following attribute:
* year, a number
* month, a number between 1 and 12
* day, a number between 1 and 31
* year, month and day are mandatory if birthday exists

Title: JSON Validate, new command
Text:
Select an example of JSON string.

On the left pane, the selected JSON string is displayed. On the right pane, you see the result of the following command:
oResult <- JSON Validate ( JSON Parse ( vJson ; * ) ; vSchema ).

Title: JSON Parse, new option
Text:
Select an example of JSON text.

On the left pane, the JSON text selected in displayed. On the right pane, you see the result of the following command:
vResult <- JSON Parse ( vJson ; * )
